title: Phase-2 contract registry still describes legacy conditional and bridge stages that the planner no longer schedules
why_it_matters: Governance/docs describe a discovery chain that does not actually execute, so operator expectations and contract audits can be wrong before runtime starts.
suggested_remediation: Either remove legacy conditional/bridge stages from the registry/docs or restore explicit scheduling paths and end-to-end planner tests.

title: Strategy packaging stages point at nonexistent wrapper scripts while contract validation still reports the plan valid
why_it_matters: A plan can pass the contract layer and then fail only at execution time when Python cannot open the stage script.
suggested_remediation: Point the planner and registry at real implementations under project/research/* or restore the missing wrappers, then make the registry validator reject nonexistent scripts.

title: README declares docs/generated authoritative, but required generated artifacts are missing and build_system_map --check fails
why_it_matters: Governance-facing generated artifacts are incomplete and not reproducible from the checked-in regeneration path, so artifact trust is lower than the docs claim.
suggested_remediation: Narrow the documented generated surface or commit and continuously check the full declared set in CI.

title: System-map ownership points to services, but run_all actually executes wrappers and CLI modules
why_it_matters: The declared boundary map is not the real orchestration graph, which hides ownership and upgrade risk.
suggested_remediation: Either make wrappers call services directly as the canonical surface or update the stage_dag/system_map to reflect the actual wrapper chain.

title: Stage contracts are enforced by naming and glob patterns rather than executable or artifact proof
why_it_matters: High-leverage failures survive planning and contract checks until runtime or later artifact review.
suggested_remediation: Add existence/import checks and at least one artifact-level smoke assertion per stage family.

title: Search evaluator counts validation events whose forward-return horizon exits in the test split
why_it_matters: Holdout statistics can incorporate future test data while still being labeled as validation, biasing discovery and promotion evidence.
suggested_remediation: Use event-window-aware split assignment or drop events whose realized horizon crosses a split boundary.

title: Shift placebo construction uses the previous event timestamp instead of a true bar-offset timestamp
why_it_matters: The placebo no longer represents a bar-shift null and can duplicate real event timing structure.
suggested_remediation: Shift by bar-duration or bar index, not by previous-row timestamp reuse.

title: Confirmatory placebo pass/fail can be driven by train rows because placebo frames are not restricted to evaluation splits
why_it_matters: Discovery-vs-confirmatory separation collapses inside the falsification path.
suggested_remediation: Filter placebo frames to evaluation rows before placebo gating.

title: Run comparison treats explicit zero-valued split knobs as unavailable instead of mismatches
why_it_matters: Runs with materially different purge/embargo/entry-lag contracts can compare as compatible.
suggested_remediation: Treat explicit zero as meaningful and compare presence separately from value.

title: Promotion output assembly can silently drop malformed evidence bundles without failing closed
why_it_matters: A promoted candidate can survive while its evidence bundle artifact is missing or malformed.
suggested_remediation: Make promotion assembly fail when promoted rows lack valid evidence bundles, or emit a blocking integrity error on count mismatch.

title: Confirmatory candidate matching only preserves economic identity when optional fields happen to be present
why_it_matters: Two runs can match as structurally continuous without proving they used the same cost contract.
suggested_remediation: Promote economic-identity fields from optional to required for confirmatory matching, or downgrade comparisons to non-confirmatory when they are absent.

title: Certification mode records strict artifact-safety flags but never enables the environment gates that enforce them
why_it_matters: Certification mode advertises fail-closed artifact isolation while still allowing the default fallback behavior.
suggested_remediation: Export the enforcement env vars from run_all in certification mode and add an execution-path test that proves the stricter behavior is active.

title: build_features can exit success with no inputs, no outputs, and no per-symbol results
why_it_matters: A green feature stage can represent zero produced artifacts, which is a false-green path and breaks cache/reconciliation logic.
suggested_remediation: Make zero-produced-symbol runs fail or warn, and require successful manifests to declare existing outputs.

title: ingest_binance_um_ohlcv converts total fetch failure into a green stage with only missing-archive stats
why_it_matters: The root raw-data producer can go green when no requested market data was actually ingested.
suggested_remediation: Treat coverage failure as terminal when required partitions are missing and record actual written partitions into manifest outputs.

title: Funding ingestion records missing coverage but still returns success, allowing partial or empty funding artifacts to masquerade as complete
why_it_matters: A run can appear funding-aware while silently dropping funding-derived signal content.
suggested_remediation: Differentiate complete success from partial coverage and add configurable enforcement when funding-derived features are expected.

title: Run-manifest reconciliation upgrades failed runs to success without checking declared outputs or artifact existence
why_it_matters: Manual or stale manifests can flip a failed run to success even when artifacts are missing or empty.
suggested_remediation: Require reconciliation to validate declared outputs against disk and, ideally, against the artifact contract before promoting the run.

title: Alias event ids bypass canonicalization in ontology deconfliction
why_it_matters: Equivalent events can survive as separate canonical episodes, corrupting regime routing and episode aggregation.
suggested_remediation: Normalize through resolve_event_alias() before bundle lookup and add alias/canonical regression tests.

title: ontology_audit.json is false-green: it reports no failures while every state source event is missing relative to the checked registry
why_it_matters: Operators can see a passing ontology audit when the same artifact proves state-to-event linkage is invalid against its claimed canonical source.
suggested_remediation: Validate against the unified/compiled registry instead, or escalate non-empty states_with_missing_source_event into failures.

title: Dead interaction-family specs are auto-registered as detectors outside the active ontology contract
why_it_matters: The detector registry exposes callable ids outside the authoritative ontology, creating inconsistent runtime universes depending on which registry surface code consults.
suggested_remediation: Gate interaction registration on compiled-registry membership or promote the intended interaction ids into the authoritative registry/mapping.

title: Generated ontology audits disagree on active-event count because one still relies on top-level per-event YAML presence
why_it_matters: Repo inventory artifacts disagree about the live event universe, breaking audit comparability and governance trust.
suggested_remediation: Derive both audits from the same unified runtime source or require a per-event YAML for every active event and fail when one is missing.

title: Serialized blueprint lineage is silently reset when runtime rebuilds a blueprint
why_it_matters: Artifact identity and provenance are lost exactly at the blueprint/spec -> runtime boundary.
suggested_remediation: Round-trip the full LineageSpec payload or validate the entire lineage dict directly instead of cherry-picking fields.

title: Runtime hard-requires funding_rate_scaled even for strategies that never reference funding
why_it_matters: Legal strategies can fail at runtime because the runtime contract is broader than the declared trigger/condition set.
suggested_remediation: Make funding fields lazy or defaultable for strategies that do not use funding conditions.

title: Post-compile clustering is nondeterministic because it uses Python hash()
why_it_matters: The same inputs can keep or drop different blueprints across separate runs.
suggested_remediation: Use a stable digest or the explicit tuple as the clustering key.

title: Main blueprint compiler bypasses its own selection helper and ignores several exposed gating arguments
why_it_matters: Operator-facing CLI knobs appear to control compilation but do not change the output set.
suggested_remediation: Route main() through _choose_event_rows() or delete the dead CLI knobs.

title: spec/strategies YAML files are not part of the executable pipeline and have drifted from runtime reality
why_it_matters: These YAML files look authoritative but do not govern runtime behavior.
suggested_remediation: Either wire these YAMLs into an enforced compile path or demote them to examples/tests and point operators at Blueprint/ExecutableStrategySpec as the source of truth.

title: Monitor-only runners can still send real flatten orders when the kill-switch fires
why_it_matters: A supposedly non-trading observer can place real venue orders during a stale-feed or health-triggered unwind.
suggested_remediation: Hard-gate all venue mutation paths on runtime_mode, or rename/document the mode if protective order placement is intentional.

title: Monitor-only startup still requires a tradable Binance account and trading credentials
why_it_matters: There is no safe read-only deployment tier despite the naming and config contract.
suggested_remediation: Branch startup requirements on runtime_mode and allow monitor_only to use read-only credentials/connectivity checks.

title: Incubation or paper gating is ineffective and graduation status is inverted
why_it_matters: Incubating strategies are not truly kept out of live trading, and explicit graduation status is misreported.
suggested_remediation: Fix is_graduated() semantics and enforce incubation at the OMS submission boundary.

title: Any caller can forge a StrategyResult and reach live trading by flipping strategy_runtime.implemented
why_it_matters: Unsafe or fabricated runtime objects can bypass provenance checks and create venue orders.
suggested_remediation: Bind live-trading eligibility to validated runtime object type or signed strategy provenance, not a free-form implemented flag.

title: Runtime postflight audit can return pass for malformed event rows because normalization is skipped
why_it_matters: Replay/postflight artifacts can go green on malformed runtime data that the real normalization stage would reject or truncate.
suggested_remediation: Normalize first or consume the normalized replay artifact before performing postflight checks.

title: OMS fills do not update LiveStateStore, so safety decisions run on stale account state until the next snapshot sync
why_it_matters: Sizing, drawdown checks, and kill-switch logic can act on stale balances and positions between exchange syncs.
suggested_remediation: Apply provisional local state updates on fills or maintain a merged local execution ledger for safety-critical logic.

title: Execution cost units diverge across core, eval, and research paths
why_it_matters: The same fee config produces different after-cost returns depending on the path that computed them.
suggested_remediation: Adopt one repo-wide cost unit contract and rename fields/sites so per-side and round-trip values cannot be confused.

title: Funding carry can make stressed-cost robustness improve as costs are stressed
why_it_matters: A cost-stress panel can certify fragile candidates as more robust when stressed harder.
suggested_remediation: Stress transaction costs separately from funding carry and clamp transaction-cost inputs to non-negative values.

title: Funding carry is applied as a flat trade adjustment independent of holding horizon
why_it_matters: A 5-minute hold and a 1-hour hold can receive identical funding carry, distorting horizon comparisons.
suggested_remediation: Accrue carry over the actual holding window using aligned funding timestamps and interval counts.

title: Research helper integrate_execution_costs is broken and does not call the shared core utility correctly
why_it_matters: A purported shared research integration path fails immediately and cannot share semantics with the maintained core path.
suggested_remediation: Delete the dead helper or rewrite it against the actual resolve_execution_costs() keyword contract.

title: The repo marks after-cost outputs as including funding carry even when actual carry coverage can be zero
why_it_matters: Consumers can assume net expectancy already includes carry economics when the observed coverage is actually zero.
suggested_remediation: Derive the flag from observed coverage or split formula capability from observed carry coverage.

title: Compiled registry ignores the declared default entry-lag grid and falls back to (1, 2)
why_it_matters: Wildcard/default lag expansion under-enumerates the declared search space and drops lag 3.
suggested_remediation: Read defaults.param_grids.common.entry_lag_bars (or support both keys during migration) and add a regression test for [1,2,3].

title: Direct experiment-config path accepts zero-lag hypotheses that search validators classify as leakage
why_it_matters: The experiment-engine entrypoint can generate same-bar-leakage hypotheses that the core search validator would reject.
suggested_remediation: Enforce entry_lag >= 1 in experiment_engine_validators and normalize existing configs away from zero.

title: Authoritative search-limit defaults advertise invalid zero-lag values that the proposal pipeline rejects
why_it_matters: The same registry file is presented as authoritative defaults but cannot survive the proposal path that consumes it.
suggested_remediation: Remove 0 from search_limits.yaml or change the downstream leakage contract; the mixed state is self-invalidating.

title: carry_state=neutral is declared as allowed in one spec but rejected by the compiled runtime context map
why_it_matters: Proposal/search/runtime semantics for carry_state are internally contradictory.
suggested_remediation: Add neutral to the compiled context map or remove it from the allowed-values surface.

title: Experiment-engine registry consistency check is effectively unreachable because it reads the wrong registry shapes
why_it_matters: The runtime safeguard is a no-op, so drift can be caught in one test stack but never by the direct runtime path.
suggested_remediation: Point the runtime check at the same authoritative spec files as the CI consistency test, or remove the dead runtime check.

title: cost_profiles and conditioning_intersections are declared in search specs but have no runtime consumers
affected_files: spec/search_space.yaml; spec/search/search_full.yaml; spec/search/search_phase1.yaml; spec/search/search_phase2.yaml; spec/search/search_phase3.yaml; spec/search/search_synthetic_truth.yaml
why_it_matters: Operator-facing knobs appear authoritative but currently do nothing.
suggested_remediation: Implement consumers or reject/deprecate these unsupported keys in schema validation.

title: CLI smoke entrypoint silently ignores --seed and --storage_mode
why_it_matters: The maintained smoke CLI does not honor its own operator-facing contract, and tier2 only exercises defaults.
suggested_remediation: Pass the parsed args into run_smoke_cli() and add a CLI-level contract test.

title: Synthetic-truth validation in CI is mostly artifact-plumbing validation, not full inference-chain validation
why_it_matters: A stale or fabricated report can satisfy CI without proving detector inference worked on the synthetic input.
suggested_remediation: Add one deterministic end-to-end canary that runs the real detector path and validates the real emitted report without monkeypatching.

title: PR CI tiers do not gate several high-risk deterministic surfaces before merge
why_it_matters: Breakage in smoke orchestration, synthetic-truth wiring, and PIT invariants can merge without PR-time detection.
suggested_remediation: Promote a minimal subset of tier2 into PR gating: CLI smoke, one synthetic-truth canary, and one PIT canary.

title: PIT and promotion safety helper modules exist but are entirely outside test and CI coverage
why_it_matters: Either critical PIT/promotion safety guards are unverified, or dead code creates a false sense of protection.
suggested_remediation: Add narrow deterministic unit tests or remove/deprecate the unused safety-helper surfaces.

title: Promotion smoke test contains stale defect commentary that contradicts current runtime behavior
why_it_matters: Stale defect commentary distorts audit and maintenance work even when runtime behavior has improved.
suggested_remediation: Remove/update the stale comment and add an explicit promotion-mode smoke test.

title: Benchmark tools write to two different artifact roots, making direct matrix runs invisible to review tooling
why_it_matters: An operator can run the documented benchmark path successfully and then fail to discover or compare the result using maintained review tooling.
suggested_remediation: Unify benchmark roots through one shared path helper and add a default-discoverability regression test.

title: edge-live-engine has undocumented environment contracts and a dead-end default config
why_it_matters: A documented maintained entry point is not operable from its own visible CLI contract.
suggested_remediation: Fail fast when no runtime environment resolves, document the full env contract, and remove or replace the dead-end default config.

title: run_researcher_verification hardcodes direct phase-2 paths and rejects supported nested search_engine artifact layouts
why_it_matters: A verification tool rejects valid outputs because it bypasses the repo’s shared artifact resolver.
suggested_remediation: Use project.artifacts.catalog helpers in _verify_experiment_artifacts() and add tests for both direct and nested layouts.

title: Two cleanup entry points with the same purpose have materially different destructive behavior
why_it_matters: Two similarly named cleaners can preserve history in one invocation and destructively wipe broader trees in another.
suggested_remediation: Collapse to one maintained cleaner or document/rename the destructive differences prominently.

title: One-off mutation scripts bypass proposal/governance contracts and edit tracked code or config directly
why_it_matters: These scripts circumvent the disciplined operator surfaces the docs tell users to trust and can silently desynchronize registries/specs/generated artifacts.
suggested_remediation: Remove them from the maintained tree or fold them into a governed tool that supports --check/--apply and post-mutation validation.

title: strategy_workbench.py is an undocumented bypass path that writes temp specs and launches run_all directly
why_it_matters: Operators can discover a plausible-looking script that bypasses the repo’s claimed planning and memory/accountability flow.
suggested_remediation: Retire it or convert it into a proposal generator that delegates through issue_proposal/execute_proposal.

title: Legacy memory rebuild scripts are hardcoded to one synthetic run and mutate persistent memory tables with no controls
why_it_matters: They are operator-confusing legacy surfaces that can overwrite persistent memory state for one hardcoded program.
suggested_remediation: Delete them if obsolete or replace them with parameterized CLIs requiring explicit ids and apply confirmation.

title: Artifact regeneration script hardcodes python3 instead of the project interpreter
why_it_matters: Artifact regeneration can fail or drift on hosts where python3 is not the project environment.
suggested_remediation: Resolve the project interpreter explicitly or move artifact regeneration behind a make target/Python entry point that uses the configured env.
